question.text: When an option expires worthless (out-of-the-money), the accounting treatment for the buyer is:

answer.text: Debit: Loss on Options Trading Account; Credit: Option Premium Paid Account (the premium is written off as a loss)
No entry is required as the option simply ceases to exist
Debit: Bank Account; Credit: Option Premium Account (premium is refunded)
Debit: Option Premium Account; Credit: Reserve Account

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When an option expires OTM: For the Buyer: The premium paid (asset) is written off as a loss. Entry: Debit: Loss on Options Trading; Credit: Option Premium Paid Account
For the Writer: The premium received (liability) is recognized as income. Entry: Debit: Option Premium Received Account; Credit: Profit on Options Trading
**The Trap:** An entry IS required - the premium asset must be written off. The premium is NOT refunded on expiry.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Expiry/exercise accounting entries are tested for both buyers and sellers.
**Key Takeaway:** OTM expiry: Buyer writes off premium as loss. Writer recognizes premium as income. Both need journal entries.
